label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is comparing the new knee device with Larry Rome's 2005 bag. The options are A, B, C, D. First, I need to recall what each device does. The new knee device is attached to the knee and captures energy when the person slows down while walking. The text says that with this device, an adult walking quickly could produce 13 watts in a minute. But the bag by Larry Rome requires the walker to carry a load of 20-30 kg. So the key difference here is about adding load. The new device doesn't add a heavy load because it's attached to the knee. The bag, on the other hand, requires carrying a heavy load. The question is asking what the new device does compared to the bag. Looking at the options: A says "produces power without adding more loads to the walker." That seems correct because the bag adds a load, but the new device doesn't. Option B says "can produce more power in a much shorter time." Wait, the text says the new device produces 13 watts in a minute. The bag is said to produce more electricity than the knee device. So B is incorrect because the new device doesn't produce more power. Option C: "needs to be equipped with a battery." The text doesn't mention a battery for either device, so probably not C. Option D: "can help the walker walk faster." The text mentions that walking quickly produces more power, but the device itself doesn't
